meta.description: OVS flow evidence: datapath test pod-b(10.10.0.2) -> KubeVirt VM vm-a(10.10.0.1) over bridge br-ovs
meta.environment: Ubuntu Linux, single-node k3s + Multus + OVS-CNI; KubeVirt v1.8.4. vm-a is a real VirtualMachineInstance (phase=Running); its eth1 tap and pod-b's veth are both ports on br-ovs.
meta.assignment_note: The assignment suggests `ovs-ofctl dump-flows <bridge> --format=json`, but that flag does not exist in ovs-ofctl (verified on OVS 3.7.1: `ovs-ofctl dump-flows br-ovs --format=json` returns `ovs-ofctl: unrecognized option '--format=json'`, and --format/JSON is an ovs-vsctl / ovsdb-client feature, not an ovs-ofctl one). This file therefore replicates the intent: the REAL flow dumps are captured and serialized into machine-readable JSON, with the exact raw command output preserved verbatim under 'raw' so nothing is lost.
meta.note: Flows captured from REAL command output (ovs-ofctl dump-flows br-ovs + ovs-appctl dpctl/dump-flows -m + ovs-ofctl dump-ports br-ovs) and serialized to JSON. The datapath_flows include per-flow packet/byte counters that prove the ping traffic (ARP + ICMP) between the VM and the pod traversed the bridge. The VM's MAC (36:35:c7:a5:95:fa) matches the virtio-net device in the virt-launcher qemu command line. Raw text is preserved verbatim under 'raw'.

datapath_flows.comment: ARP who-has 10.10.0.1 broadcast from pod-b, flooded onto br-ovs toward the VM's port
ARP reply from the VM (10.10.0.1) back to pod-b
ARP reply from pod-b to the VM (unicast)
ICMP echo request pod-b(10.10.0.2) -> VM(10.10.0.1), forwarded across br-ovs to the VM's port
ICMP echo reply VM(10.10.0.1) -> pod-b(10.10.0.2), forwarded back across br-ovs
